title: Profile corrections handoff + lock-in (profile-corrections block, AGENTS.md routing-table row)

impl.notes: context/profile.md was already on line 3 of .gitignore (AC4 pre-satisfied). buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown is file-local (not in handoff.ts) so the 'profile-corrections' kind string appears directly in onboarding.tsx, satisfying the grep check. The Ask text uses the exact sensitivity gate phrase from AGENTS.md §Sensitivity check and includes the first-person conversion step from templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md §Follow-up prompt.

verificationRun.result: PASS — matched: | Tier 2 | `context/profile.md` | Full AI context profile (bootstrap path) | Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md |
PASS — ✓ built in 352ms / 328ms (two passes, no errors)
PASS — prettier ran; onboarding.tsx reformatted; build re-verified clean

userStory: As a user who has reviewed my profile sections, I want a corrections handoff block that sends only my edits back to the assistant, so that the assistant applies them and distils my OS files without me re-sending unchanged content.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Phase 4 (seeded path) Collect & Copy button emits a block whose first line is exactly `## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <YYYY-MM-DD>` (today's date at copy time), followed by ONLY the edited sections (those with the 'edited' badge in story 010). The lock-in ask is included: apply corrections to context/profile.md, then distil context/me.md, context/org.md (sensitivity gate: no personnel data, unreleased roadmap items, or commercial terms in the committed org.md), and context/active.md seeds from it.
If zero sections were edited, the block states "No corrections — all sections accepted as is" (no section content is emitted) and the lock-in ask still appears.

`AGENTS.md` gains a routing-table row for `context/profile.md` with the when-to-load value: "Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md". `grep 'context/profile.md' AGENTS.md` returns a match. This row is added by the implementing agent as part of this story — not by the wizard.
`grep 'context/profile.md' .gitignore` returns a match — context/profile.md is gitignored and will never be committed.

After the assistant responds to the corrections handoff and distils me.md and org.md, `cat context/me.md` shows a `# Me · Updated: <today>` header with no `[bracketed]` placeholders — confirming the assistant wrote the file from the profile content, not a scaffold template.

technicalNotes: Wire the Collect & Copy button in the PhaseReviewCorrect component (story 010) to emit the profile-corrections block using the HandoffDock pattern. Block body is built from `answers.editedSections` in onboarding-state.json (story 010) — iterate over keys, include only entries where `edited: true`. Add `context/profile.md` to `.gitignore` (check it is not already covered by an existing glob pattern). Implement the AGENTS.md routing-table addition as a direct file edit (this is the only AGENTS.md change in this story). The sensitivity gate wording in the lock-in ask must match the exact phrase from `AGENTS.md` §Sensitivity check standing order. The lock-in ask also directs the assistant to apply the follow-up first-person conversion prompt from templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md (rewrites the corrected profile as polished first-person assistant context with a closing 'What I want my AI assistant to optimize for' section) before distilling me/org/active.
outOfScope: The actual AI execution of distillation (the wizard cannot read the chat), the scaffold-files function writing active.md from phase 5 answers (story 004), os-helper handling of the profile-corrections handoff kind (story 006), Health tile (story 008).

verificationPlan.checks: Returns a match confirming the corrections handoff kind is wired in the phase 4 seeded-path Collect & Copy.
Returns a match confirming context/profile.md is explicitly gitignored.
Returns a match confirming the new routing-table row for context/profile.md is present in AGENTS.md.

revisionLog.change: Lock-in flow incorporates the production follow-up prompt (first-person conversion) from the bootstrap template.
revisionLog.reason: OQ-005 resolved — production prompt package includes a tested first-person conversion step for self-service onboarding.
